rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGH ELWOOD MACBETH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/01/2021. Third continuance for same issues. 1) Please explain why the petition alleges Decedent died September 12, 2019. The petition for probate and Order for Probate state the Decedent died September 14, 2019. 2) A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.35]. 3) The I&A alleges under #4 by order of the court dated (specify) 12/23/2019 [No Sch 2 Items]. Please explain the order of the court dated 12/23/2019 appointed Karen Ann Simons as referee. 4) There is no attached detailed schedules of receipts. 1 total amount for all the receipts is not detailed and must be amended. When an account has been waived, the report must contain the information required by rule 7.550. If the report is accompanied by a request for statutory commissions or fees, the basis for their computation must be included in the petition substantially in the form provided in (a). Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705]. 5) Please explain why the calculation of statutory fees allege $102.10 in additional interest accrued when the account alleges $90.86 in interest. 6) Please explain the calculation of statutory fees. The Final I&A alleges $318,591.62. The account alleges $90.86 in interest. However, the calculation alleges $102.10 in interest. Please explain how $319,693.72 is the basis for the calculations of statutory compensation. 7) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] 8) Please explain who High E. Macbeth III is and why High E. Macbeth III listed as an heir of Decedent on Exhibit C and person entitled to notice on Exhibit D. 9) The prayer on page 11 of the petition does not explain how the estate is to be distributed. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. 10) The proposed order must match what is requested in the petition. The proposed order page 5 #4 is not in the prayer of the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/15/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN JOSEPH GRIFFIN JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The first and final account of Michael Edward Kahn, as executor, is allowed except for the $200 consultation fee, $7,500.00 paid for trust administration with no legal authority which has been paid back to the estate and for failure to keep over $250,000.00 in a single account over the FDIC limit. All reported acts and transactions of Michael Edward Kahn, as executor,, as set forth in his Petition are accepted. The Executor, Michael Edward Kahn, is hereby authorized and directed to pay to himself the sum of $36,235.45 as statutory compensation for services rendered in administering the Will, from the Estate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL BRAUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) The proof of service shows notice was mailed before the petition was filed. See LRSF 14.14(A). 3) Item 1 of the petition does not specify the newspaper name. 4) Item 3a: Please provide a copy of the death certificate, as the competing petition alleges a different date of death. 5) Item 7b(1) was checked, but item 3d shows no value of real property as an asset of this probate estate. 6) Please explain why item 7b(2) was not checked and why Probate Code section 6402.5(b) should not apply. 7) Is Alyssa Rivera an adult? 8) Please provide the date of death of Emma Alicia Kirkham. 9) Notice must be given to Nanette Meek at her place of business or residence. Probate Code section 1215(a)(3). 10) A declaration describing efforts to locate David Charney must be filed, as required by CRC 7.52. 11) Please provide the date of death of Annamae Braun's father and confirm if George Kirkham is the only sibling of Annamae Braun. 12) Leah Gomez's relationship to the decedent and her age (if minor) must be specified. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) The proof of service of notice of the second amended petition does not show the date of mailing, which must be on or after 10/05/2021 (when the filing of the second amended petition was processed and backdated by the clerk). 2) Direct notice of the second amended petition must be given to James Chan, in addition to his attorney of record. 3) No proof of publication of the second amended petition is on file. Published notice must specifically refer to the "second amended" petition. 4) The proposed personal representative's consent to serve is not on file. LRSF 14.24. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLENN BARON GETMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed and whether a proof of holographic instrument signed by someone who is not petitioner is required. On page 2 of the petition #3(f) petitioner checked the will and all codicils are self-proving. The Will is not self-proving under Probate Code 8220 and CCP 2015.5. In response petitioner signed a proof of holographic instrument. 2) Caption of petition does not indicate whether full or limited authority is sought. Page 1 #2.(c) requests full authority. Proof of publication requested full authority.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEITH MICHAEL MORSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/1/2021 at request of counsel. Why this probate is necessary when the estate is under $166,250.00? (See Probate Code 13100 et seq.) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.
